name: Chicken Basque- Delia Smith's Famous Recipe
recipeIngredient: 5 ounces chorizo, skinned, cut into 1/2-inch slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the chicken pieces with salt and pepper.
2. Slice the red peppers in half and remove the seeds and pith; slice each half into six strips. Peel the onions and slice into strips of approximately the same size. Drain the dried tomatoes, wipe dry with paper towels, then cut into 1/2-inch pieces.
3. Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a casserole. When hot, add chicken pieces (2-3 at a time) and brown to a nutty golden color on both sides. Using a slotted spoon, remove them to a plate lined with paper towels.
4. Add more oil to the casserole over medium heat. Cook onion and peppers for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally until they begin to brown at the edges.
5. Add garlic, chorizo, and dried tomatoes; toss for a minute or two until garlic is pale golden and chorizo has light color.
6. Stir in rice; when grains are well coated with oil, add stock, wine, tomato paste, and paprika. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to a gentle simmer.
7. Add more seasoning, place chicken gently on top (keep rice below liquid), sprinkle herbs over pieces, scatter olives and orange wedges among them.
8. Cover tightly and cook over the gentlest possible heat for 50 minutes to 1 hour, or until rice is cooked but retains some bite. Alternatively, bake in a preheated oven at 350°F for 1 hour.
Serves 4.
